description_long: This is a large, cylindrical wooden barrel with a weathered, rust-stained exterior, giving it an aged and rugged appearance. The barrel features a prominent blue painted band around its midsection, partially covered by white text that reads 'PROPERTY OF HER MAJESTY THE QUEEN.' A large, detailed Union Jack flag is painted on one side, further emphasizing its British association. The top surface appears to be flat and metallic, with visible signs of rust and wear, suggesting prolonged outdoor exposure. The staves of the barrel are reinforced with horizontal bands, likely metal, that are also rusted. The overall look suggests it was used for official or military storage, possibly as a prop or decorative object in an urban or historical setting.
description: A large, weathered wooden barrel with a blue band, British flag, and white official text, featuring rusted metal bands and a flat, worn top.
description_view_0: Front view showing the blue band with partial text and the start of the Union Jack flag.
description_view_1: Side view with the blue band and more of the white text 'OF HER MAJESTY THE QUEEN' visible.
description_view_2: Rear side view showing the end of the blue band and a large portion of the Union Jack flag.
description_view_3: Back view dominated by the Union Jack flag and the weathered wood texture.
attribute_labeling_or_markings: blue band with white text 'PROPERTY OF HER MAJESTY THE QUEEN', Union Jack flag
